examples.title: Algorithm: Tomcat WAR Deployment Process
examples.description: Steps Tomcat follows when deploying a web application from a WAR file
examples.code:
ALGORITHM DeployWebApp
BEGIN
 1. Locate WAR file in Tomcat's webapps directory
 2. Extract WAR contents into a subdirectory
 3. Parse WEB-INF/web.xml for servlet configurations
 4. Load servlet classes specified in <servlet-class>
 5. Initialize servlets by calling init() method
 6. Map URL patterns to corresponding servlets
 7. Mark application as available for requests
END
examples.explanation: Tomcat automatically deploys WAR files by extracting contents, parsing deployment descriptors, loading servlet classes, and initializing them. Step 6 handles URL mapping from web.xml.

examples.title: Basic Servlet Structure with doGet
examples.description: Simple servlet handling GET requests using Jakarta Servlet API
examples.explanation: Extends HttpServlet and overrides doGet method. Sets content type to HTML and writes response using PrintWriter. Tomcat manages the servlet lifecycle.

examples.title: Algorithm: Servlet Request Handling Flow
examples.description: Tomcat's request processing workflow for servlets
examples.code:
ALGORITHM HandleRequest
BEGIN
 1. Receive HTTP request from client
 2. Determine target servlet from URL mapping
 3. Check if servlet is loaded & initialized
 4. Create HttpServletRequest/Response objects
 5. Call servlet's service() method
 6. Process business logic in doGet/doPost
 7. Send rendered response to client
END
examples.explanation: Tomcat manages the entire request lifecycle. Steps 4-7 demonstrate container responsibilities. service() method delegates to appropriate HTTP method handlers.

examples.title: Reading Servlet Parameters
examples.description: Accessing context and init parameters in servlets
examples.explanation: Demonstrates two parameter types: context parameters (application-wide) via ServletContext, and servlet-specific init parameters. Retrieved during initialization phase.
